faqs.question: Why is eating together with family important?
faqs.answer: Eating together is important because it is a time when the whole family sits in one place, shares food, and talks to each other. It makes family bonds stronger. Children learn good eating habits by watching elders. It also makes sure everyone in the family eats proper food. In many Indian families, eating together is a special tradition that has been followed for generations.

faqs.question: Why do people in different parts of India eat different foods?
faqs.answer: People eat different foods based on three main reasons: (1) What grows in their area - South India grows more rice, so people eat more rice dishes. North India grows wheat, so people eat roti. (2) Climate - hot areas eat lighter food, cold areas eat heavier food. (3) Culture and religion - different communities have different food traditions passed down from their grandparents.

faqs.question: What is a thali and why does it have so many dishes?
faqs.answer: A thali is a round plate with small bowls (katoris) holding different dishes - dal, sabzi, roti or rice, pickle, papad, curd, and sometimes a sweet. It has so many dishes because each one gives different nutrients to our body. The thali is a perfect example of a balanced meal! Different states have their own style of thali - like Gujarati thali, Rajasthani thali, and South Indian meals on banana leaf.

faqs.question: What special foods are made during Diwali?
faqs.answer: During Diwali, families make many delicious sweets and snacks. Popular sweets include laddoo, barfi, gulab jamun, jalebi, and kaju katli. Savoury snacks like mathri, namak pare, chakli, and murukku are also prepared. Many families make these at home together, which is a fun activity. The sweets are shared with neighbours and friends as a way of spreading happiness!

faqs.question: What is a langar and who can eat there?
faqs.answer: A langar is a free community kitchen at a Sikh gurudwara. Anyone can eat there - rich or poor, any religion or caste. Everyone sits on the floor in a line and eats the same simple food together. The food is cooked and served by volunteers. The langar at the Golden Temple in Amritsar feeds about 1 lakh (100,000) people every day! It teaches us that food should be shared equally.

faqs.question: What is the Onam Sadhya feast?
faqs.answer: Onam Sadhya is a grand vegetarian feast served during the Onam festival in Kerala. It is served on a banana leaf and can have 26 or more different dishes! The dishes are placed in a specific order on the leaf. It includes rice, sambar, avial, payasam (sweet pudding), pickles, and many vegetable dishes. Everyone in the family and neighbourhood eats together, making it a very special occasion.

faqs.question: Why do some people not eat certain foods?
faqs.answer: People do not eat certain foods for different reasons. Some families are vegetarian because of their religion or personal belief. Some people are allergic to certain foods like nuts or milk. During religious fasting, people avoid some foods for a few days. Some people do not eat certain animals because they respect them. We should always respect what others choose to eat or not eat.

faqs.question: What does Indian hospitality mean when it comes to food?
faqs.answer: In India, when a guest comes to your house, the first thing we do is offer them water and food. There is a saying in Sanskrit - 'Atithi Devo Bhava' which means 'the guest is like God'. Even if the guest comes unexpectedly, Indian families try their best to share whatever food they have. This tradition of welcoming guests with food has been part of Indian culture for thousands of years!

faqs.question: Why should we not waste food?
faqs.answer: We should not waste food because many people in India and the world do not have enough to eat. Farmers work very hard to grow food - they plough fields, sow seeds, water crops, and wait for months before the food is ready. Wasting food means wasting all their hard work, water, and resources. Take only as much food as you can eat, and if there is leftover food, share it with someone who is hungry.

faqs.question: What are some common Indian spices used in cooking?
faqs.answer: Indian food uses many spices that give it special flavour and colour. Common spices include turmeric (haldi) which gives yellow colour, red chilli powder for spiciness, cumin (jeera) for flavour, coriander (dhaniya) powder, garam masala (a mix of spices), mustard seeds, and cardamom (elaichi) for sweets. India is called the 'Land of Spices' because so many spices grow here!
